docs.title: Meridian
docs.text: Shared memory for your AI sessions.

docs.title: The Problem
docs.text:
Every AI coding session starts completely fresh. Open Claude Code in two terminals and they have absolutely no idea what the other is doing. Hit the context limit mid-task and your entire working memory evaporates. Run parallel sessions and they step on each other — duplicate work, conflicting edits, no coordination.

This isn't a Claude problem. It's a fundamental limitation of stateless sessions. And it gets worse as you use AI more.

docs.title: The Solution
docs.text:
Meridian is a local MCP server that gives all your Claude sessions a shared persistent brain.

Every session that connects to Meridian can:

  • Read the current goal — what the project is trying to accomplish right now
  • Log tasks — what it's doing, what it finished, what failed
  • See every other session's work — no duplicate effort
  • Generate instant handoffs — compressed context files that resume a new session in seconds

When context fills up, generate a handoff and start fresh. The new session reads the file and picks up exactly where you left off — no re-explaining.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     Your machine                        │
│                                                         │
│  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  │
│  │ Claude Code  │  │  Claude      │  │   Cursor /   │  │
│  │  terminal 1  │  │  Desktop     │  │  Windsurf    │  │
│  └──────┬───────┘  └──────┬───────┘  └──────┬───────┘  │
│         │                 │                 │           │
│         └─────────────────┴─────────────────┘           │
│                           │ MCP stdio                    │
│                    ┌──────▼───────┐                     │
│                    │   Meridian   │                     │
│                    │  MCP Server  │                     │
│                    │  :7878       │                     │
│                    └──────┬───────┘                     │
│                           │                             │
│                    ┌──────▼───────┐                     │
│                    │  SQLite DB   │  ← or Postgres      │
│                    │  meridian.db │     (Neon / any)    │
│                    └──────────────┘                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

For hosted tier (usemeridian.us), Meridian runs in the cloud and each workspace gets an isolated Neon Postgres database. Your Claude sessions connect over HTTPS with a bearer token — zero local install required.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature What it does start_session Register this session, load full context in one call get_goal Read the shared north star, sprint, and version goal log_task Log progress — all sessions see it instantly claim_task Lock a task so two sessions can't double-work it generate_handoff Compress full context into a resumable file get_sprint_items See the sprint board — what's todo, in progress, done set_goal Update the shared goal — all sessions align instantly get_sessions See all active sessions and their last activity

docs.title: Quick Install
docs.text:
pixi (recommended)pipDocker 
git clone https://github.com/meridianmcp/Meridian
cd Meridian
pixi install
pixi run start

git clone https://github.com/meridianmcp/Meridian
cd Meridian
pip install -e .
python -m meridian

git clone https://github.com/meridianmcp/Meridian
cd Meridian
docker compose up

Then add to your Claude Code .mcp.json:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "meridian": {
      "command": "pixi",
      "args": ["run", "python", "-m", "meridian", "--mcp"],
      "cwd": "/path/to/Meridian"
    }
  }
}

→ Full quickstart guide

docs.title: Hosted Tier
docs.text:
Don't want to run your own server? usemeridian.us is a hosted version — sign in with Google or GitHub, get a managed Neon Postgres database, and connect over HTTPS.

$20/month — 7-day free trial, no commitment.

→ Hosted tier guide

docs.title: HTTP API Reference
docs.text:
Meridian exposes a REST API at http://localhost:7878 (local) or https://usemeridian.us (hosted).

Most endpoints are for internal use by the dashboard and MCP layer. The MCP tools are the recommended interface for AI sessions. This reference is for developers building integrations.

docs.title: GET /health
docs.text:
Check server health.

Auth: None required

Response: 

{
  "status": "ok",
  "version": "1.9.0",
  "db": "postgres"
}

docs.title: GET /projects
docs.text:
List all projects.

Auth: None (local) / Session cookie or Bearer token (hosted)

Response: [{id, name, created_at, ...}]

docs.title: POST /projects
docs.text:
Create a project.

Body: 

{"name": "backend-api"}

Response (201): 

{"id": "uuid", "name": "backend-api", "created_at": "2026-05-23T00:00:00Z"}

docs.title: GET /projects/{project_id}
docs.text:
Get a single project.

Response (404): {"detail": "Project not found"}

docs.title: POST /projects/{project_id}/rename
docs.text:
Rename a project.

Body: {"name": "new-name"}

docs.title: DELETE /projects/{project_id}
docs.text:
Delete a project and all its data.

Response: 204 No Content

docs.title: GET /projects/{project_id}/goal
docs.text:
Get the current goal state.

Response: 

{
  "id": "uuid",
  "project_id": "uuid",
  "content": "SHIPPED:\n...",
  "north_star": "Long-term vision...",
  "sprint": "v2.3 — this week",
  "version": 42,
  "created_at": "...",
  "updated_at": "..."
}

docs.title: POST /projects/{project_id}/goal
docs.text:
Set the goal content.

Body: 

{
  "content": "SHIPPED:\n...\nCURRENT FOCUS:\n...",
  "north_star": "optional — update north star",
  "sprint": "optional — update sprint",
  "minor": false
}

minor: true updates in place without bumping the version number (used for AUTO BLOCKS).

docs.title: POST /projects/{project_id}/goal/north-star
docs.text:
Update only the north star.

Body: {"north_star": "...", "human_id": "alice"}

docs.title: POST /projects/{project_id}/goal/sprint
docs.text:
Update only the sprint.

Body: {"sprint": "v2.3 — rate limiting + tests"}

docs.title: GET /projects/{project_id}/goal-history
docs.text:
Get all goal versions for a project. Newest first. Strips AUTO BLOCKS for clean diffs.

Response: [{version, content, north_star, sprint, created_at}]

docs.title: GET /projects/{project_id}/sessions
docs.text:
List active sessions for a project.

Response: 

[{
  "id": "uuid",
  "project_id": "uuid",
  "name": "feature-auth-fix",
  "status": "active",
  "human_id": "alice",
  "last_seen": "2026-05-23T10:00:00Z",
  "created_at": "..."
}]

docs.title: POST /sessions/register
docs.text:
Register a new session.

Body: 

{
  "project_id": "uuid",
  "name": "my-session",
  "human_id": "alice"
}

docs.title: POST /sessions/{session_id}/heartbeat
docs.text:
Update a session's last_seen timestamp.

Response: {"ok": true}

docs.title: POST /sessions/{session_id}/close
docs.text: Close a session.

docs.title: GET /projects/{project_id}/tasks
docs.text:
Get recent tasks. Newest first.

Query params: limit (default: 50)

Response: 

[{
  "id": "uuid",
  "session_id": "uuid",
  "project_id": "uuid",
  "description": "Fixed auth bug",
  "status": "done",
  "created_at": "..."
}]

docs.title: POST /tasks
docs.text:
Log a task.

Body: 

{
  "session_id": "uuid",
  "project_id": "uuid",
  "description": "Implemented rate limiting",
  "status": "done"
}

docs.title: PATCH /tasks/{task_id}
docs.text:
Update a task (status, description).

Body: {"status": "done", "description": "updated description"}

docs.title: GET /projects/{project_id}/tasks/claimable
docs.text: List unclaimed pending tasks.

docs.title: POST /projects/{project_id}/tasks/release
docs.text:
Release a claimed task.

Body: {"task_id": "uuid", "session_id": "uuid"}

docs.title: GET /projects/{project_id}/sprint-items
docs.text:
List sprint items.

Query params: status (optional filter)

docs.title: POST /projects/{project_id}/sprint-items
docs.text:
Add a sprint item.

Body: 

{
  "title": "Add rate limiting",
  "version": "v2.3",
  "group": "Performance",
  "human_id": "alice"
}

docs.title: POST /projects/{project_id}/sprint-items/{item_id}/complete
docs.text: Mark a sprint item done. Body: {"task_id": "uuid"} (optional)

docs.title: POST /projects/{project_id}/sprint-items/{item_id}/fail
docs.text: Mark failed. Body: {"reason": "..."} (optional)

docs.title: POST /projects/{project_id}/sprint-items/{item_id}/skip
docs.text: Mark skipped. Body: {"reason": "..."} (optional)

docs.title: POST /projects/{project_id}/sprint-items/{item_id}/push
docs.text: Push to future version. Body: {"to_version": "v2.4"}

docs.title: DELETE /projects/{project_id}/sprint-items/{item_id}
docs.text: Delete a sprint item.

docs.title: POST /projects/{project_id}/handoff
docs.text:
Generate a context handoff file.

Response: 

{
  "file_path": "data/my-project_handoff.md",
  "content": "---\nMERIDIAN_CONTEXT\n..."
}

docs.title: GET /auth/login
docs.text: Serves the sign-in page with Google and GitHub OAuth buttons.

docs.title: GET /auth/google/login
docs.text: Redirect to Google OAuth consent page.

docs.title: GET /auth/callback
docs.text:
Google OAuth callback — creates/updates tenant, sets session cookie.

Query params: code (from Google)

docs.title: GET /auth/github/login
docs.text: Redirect to GitHub OAuth consent page.

docs.title: GET /auth/github/callback
docs.text:
GitHub OAuth callback — creates/updates tenant, sets session cookie.

Query params: code (from GitHub)

docs.title: GET /auth/logout
docs.text: Clear session cookie, redirect to /.

docs.title: POST /mcp
docs.text:
Remote MCP endpoint — HTTP transport.

Auth: Authorization: Bearer sk_meridian_...

Rate limit: 100 requests/minute per token

Body: Standard MCP JSON-RPC request

Response: Standard MCP JSON-RPC response

docs.title: GET /admin
docs.text:
Admin dashboard. Shows active customers, churned, signups/day, Neon project count, recent signups, payment failures.

Auth: ADMIN_EMAIL Google OAuth only

docs.title: GET /admin/git-status
docs.text:
Check if the local repo is behind the remote.

Response: 

{
  "behind": 0,
  "ahead": 0,
  "branch": "main",
  "error": null
}

docs.title: GET /demo
docs.text:
Public read-only demo dashboard. Sets a demo context cookie. Exempt from SITE_PASSWORD gate.

No auth required.

docs.title: Static Pages
docs.text: Route Description GET / Landing page with pricing GET /terms Terms of Service GET /privacy Privacy Policy GET /health Health check GET /dashboard Dashboard (auth required on hosted) GET /config Server config (version, db type, etc.)

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: All Meridian configuration is via environment variables. Set them in a .env file in the project root, via shell export, or via your deployment platform's secret store.

docs.title: Database
docs.text: Variable Description Default Required MERIDIAN_DB_URL Postgres connection string. When set, Meridian uses Postgres instead of SQLite. Example: postgresql://user:pass@host/dbname — No (SQLite if unset) MERIDIAN_DB Path to the SQLite database file. Ignored when MERIDIAN_DB_URL is set. data/meridian.db No MERIDIAN_DEMO_DB_URL Postgres URL for the isolated demo database. When set, /demo uses this DB (wiped and reseeded on every restart). — No MERIDIAN_DATA_DIR Directory for data files (SQLite DB, handoff files). data/ No

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: Variable Description Default Required GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID Google OAuth app client ID. Get from console.cloud.google.com. — For hosted Google login GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET Google OAuth app client secret. — For hosted Google login GITHUB_CLIENT_ID GitHub OAuth app client ID. Get from github.com/settings/apps. — For hosted GitHub login GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET GitHub OAuth app client secret. — For hosted GitHub login SESSION_SECRET Secret key for signing session cookies (itsdangerous). Use a long random string. dev-secret-change-me Yes for production MERIDIAN_SESSION_SECRET Alias for SESSION_SECRET. Either name works. — —

docs.title: Payments (Stripe)
docs.text:
Warning

Never switch STRIPE_SECRET_KEY from test to live without thorough testing. Keep STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_... during development.

Variable Description Default Required STRIPE_SECRET_KEY Stripe secret key (sk_test_... or sk_live_...). — For billing STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET Stripe webhook signing secret (whsec_...). From the Stripe dashboard → Webhooks. — For billing STRIPE_PAYMENT_LINK URL of your Stripe Payment Link for the Standard plan. Shown as the "Get Started" button on the landing page. /auth/login No

docs.title: Email (Resend)
docs.text: Variable Description Default Required RESEND_API_KEY Resend API key for sending transactional email. If unset, email sending is silently skipped (dev mode). — For production email MERIDIAN_FROM_EMAIL Sender address for Resend emails. Meridian <noreply@usemeridian.us> No

docs.title: Neon Provisioning
docs.text: Variable Description Default Required NEON_API_KEY Neon API key for the standard-tier account (hello@usemeridian.us). Used to provision per-customer Neon databases. — For hosted standard provisioning NEON_API_KEY_PRO Neon API key for the pro-tier account (pro@usemeridian.us). Used when provisioning pro-plan customers. Not yet required. — Future — pro tier only MAX_CUSTOMERS_PER_PROJECT Maximum customer databases per Neon pool project. 8 No MAX_PROJECTS_STANDARD Hard cap on pool projects for the standard account. Provisioning blocks at this limit. 90 No MAX_PROJECTS_PRO Hard cap on pool projects for the pro account. 95 No ALERT_THRESHOLD_STANDARD Send capacity alert email when standard pool project count exceeds this. 85 No ALERT_THRESHOLD_PRO Send capacity alert email when pro pool project count exceeds this. 90 No

docs.title: App / General
docs.text: Variable Description Default Required APP_URL Public base URL of the deployment. Used in OAuth callbacks, emails, and MCP endpoint URLs. Example: https://usemeridian.us http://localhost:7878 Yes for production MERIDIAN_BASE_URL Alias for APP_URL. Either name works. — — ADMIN_EMAIL Email address for capacity alerts and admin dashboard access. — For admin features SITE_PASSWORD When set, all routes (except /health, /demo, /__gate__) require entering this password in a gate page. Used for preview deployments. — No MERIDIAN_DEMO_DB_URL (repeated from Database section) Enables the /demo route with isolated seed data. — No MERIDIAN_AFTER_LOGIN_URL Where to redirect after successful OAuth login. /dashboard No MERIDIAN_PORT HTTP port for the dashboard/API server. 7878 No MERIDIAN_HOST Host to bind the server to. 127.0.0.1 No MERIDIAN_HUMAN_ID Default human identifier for task attribution. Falls back to $USER / $USERNAME / hostname. — No MERIDIAN_AUTO_SUMMARY_INTERVAL Seconds between auto-summary cycles (background task). 600 No

docs.title: Example .env file
docs.text:
# Database — Postgres (production)
MERIDIAN_DB_URL=postgresql://neondb_owner:...@ep-xxx.us-east-2.aws.neon.tech/neondb?sslmode=require

# Demo database — isolated Neon project
MERIDIAN_DEMO_DB_URL=postgresql://neondb_owner:...@ep-yyy.us-east-2.aws.neon.tech/neondb?sslmode=require

# Auth
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=123456789.apps.googleusercontent.com
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=GOCSPX-...
GITHUB_CLIENT_ID=Ov23li...
GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET=...
SESSION_SECRET=replace-with-a-long-random-string

# Payments
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_...
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_...
STRIPE_PAYMENT_LINK=https://buy.stripe.com/...

# Email
RESEND_API_KEY=re_...

# Neon provisioning
NEON_API_KEY=...

# App
APP_URL=https://usemeridian.us
ADMIN_EMAIL=admin@example.com

Danger

Never commit .env to git. Add it to .gitignore. Meridian's .gitignore already excludes secrets.env and .env.

docs.title: Hosted Tier — usemeridian.us
docs.text:
The hosted tier gives you a managed Meridian instance with zero local setup. Sign in with Google or GitHub, get an isolated Neon Postgres database, and connect your AI clients over HTTPS in minutes.

usemeridian.us →

docs.title: Pricing
docs.text:
Standard Pro Price $20/mo $49/mo (waitlist) Trial 7-day free, card required — Neon project 1 per workspace 1 per workspace Max databases 10 50 Max compute 2 CU 4 CU Storage 0.5 GB 5 GB Compute hours/mo 100 CU-hrs 300 CU-hrs Meridian projects Unlimited Unlimited Team members Unlimited Unlimited Environments 1 (production) Multiple (prod/staging/dev) Support Email Priority email BYODB ✓ ✓ Custom subdomain — ✓

BYODB (Bring Your Own Database): paste your own Neon connection string at signup and skip the managed DB — same price, full control of your data.

docs.title: Sign Up Flow
docs.text:
1. Visit usemeridian.us and click "Get Started"
  2. Enter payment — Stripe handles billing, 7-day trial starts immediately
  3. Sign in — click the link in your welcome email, or go to /auth/login
  4. Choose Google or GitHub — sign in with either, no passwords
  5. Check your email — welcome email contains your bearer token and MCP config snippet

docs.title: Receiving Your Welcome Email
docs.text:
After signing up, you'll receive an email from noreply@usemeridian.us with:

  • Your bearer token (sk_meridian_...) — keep this private
  • Claude Code config — ready-to-paste .mcp.json snippet
  • Claude Desktop config — ready-to-paste config snippet
  • Dashboard link — https://usemeridian.us/dashboard

docs.title: Configuring Claude Code
docs.text:
Paste this into your project's .mcp.json (or ~/.claude/mcp.json for global):

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "meridian": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "mcp-remote", "https://usemeridian.us/mcp"],
      "env": {
        "BEARER_TOKEN": "sk_meridian_your_token_here"
      }
    }
  }
}

No local install required

With the hosted tier, you don't need to clone the repo or install anything locally. npx mcp-remote handles the connection transparently.

docs.title: Configuring Claude Desktop
docs.text:
Add to claude_desktop_config.json:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "meridian": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "mcp-remote", "https://usemeridian.us/mcp"],
      "env": {
        "BEARER_TOKEN": "sk_meridian_your_token_here"
      }
    }
  }
}

macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json Windows: %APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json

docs.title: Configuring Cursor
docs.text:
Add to .cursor/mcp.json:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "meridian": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "mcp-remote", "https://usemeridian.us/mcp"],
      "env": {
        "BEARER_TOKEN": "sk_meridian_your_token_here"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Dashboard Walkthrough
docs.text: After signing in at https://usemeridian.us/auth/login:

docs.title: Projects tab
docs.text:
• Create projects — one per codebase or initiative
  • Sprint board — see all sprint items, mark done/fail/push
  • Goal editor — edit the shared goal your sessions align to

docs.title: Live tab
docs.text:
• Active sessions — all connected Claude sessions with last-seen timestamp
  • Task queue — real-time feed of what every session is doing

docs.title: Rewind tab
docs.text:
• Goal history — every version of the goal, newest first
  • Activity timeline — every task, session event, and goal change
  • Charts — tasks/day per team member, sprint velocity

docs.title: Files tab
docs.text: • AGENTS.md / ROADMAP.md / DEVLOG.md — project markdown files, editable in browser

docs.title: Managing Projects
docs.text:
Create a project: In the dashboard, click ➕ New Project, enter a name.

Rename a project: Click the kebab (⋮) menu on a project card → Rename.

Delete a project: Click the kebab menu → Delete. Deletes all sessions, tasks, and goals.

Switch projects: Use the project selector in the sidebar.

docs.title: Account Settings
docs.text:
Your account page is at https://usemeridian.us/dashboard → ⚙️ Settings.

From there you can: - View your current plan - Copy your bearer token - Manage billing (Stripe portal) - Delete your account

docs.title: BYODB Setup
docs.text:
Prefer to manage your own Neon database? You can use BYODB:

  1. Create a Neon account and project
  2. Copy your connection string: postgresql://...@....neon.tech/neondb?sslmode=require
  3. At signup, choose "Bring your own database" and paste the URL

Meridian will initialize its schema in your database on first run. Your data never touches our Neon account.

docs.title: FAQ
docs.text:
Q: Can I switch from managed DB to BYODB after signup? A: Email hello@usemeridian.us — we'll migrate you manually.

Q: What happens to my data if I cancel? A: Your Neon database is deleted 28 days after payment failure. You'll receive warning emails on day 3-7 and day 14.

Q: Can multiple team members share one account? A: Yes — unlimited members on all plans. Each member signs in with their own Google/GitHub and connects to the same workspace.

Q: Is there a rate limit on the MCP endpoint? A: 100 requests per minute per bearer token. Sufficient for all normal usage patterns.

Q: I lost my bearer token. How do I get a new one? A: Email hello@usemeridian.us — we'll regenerate it.

Q: Does the hosted tier work with Cursor / Windsurf? A: Yes — any MCP-compatible client works with the remote /mcp endpoint via mcp-remote.

Q: Where is my data stored? A: Each workspace gets an isolated Neon Postgres database in aws-us-east-2 (US East). No data mixing between customers.

docs.title: MCP Tool Reference
docs.text:
Meridian exposes 19 tools over MCP. They fall into two usage patterns:

Planner sessions (claude.ai, planning work) — start_session · pin_decision · update_decision · add_note · get_context_block · generate_handoff

Executor sessions (Claude Code, Cursor, automated workers) — start_session · log_task · request_hitl · get_session_brief · generate_handoff

docs.title: start_session
docs.text:
Register a session and get the full project context (goal, sprint, recent tasks, decisions) in one call. Use this instead of register_session.

Parameter Type Required Description project_id string required session_name string required human_id string optional client string optional

Example: 

start_session(project_id="abc-123", session_name="feature-x", human_id="alice")

docs.title: get_session_brief
docs.text:
Compact session orientation (<500 tokens). Returns sprint focus, pending items, recent tasks, blocking failures, and open HITL requests. Ideal for worker/automation sessions that don't need the full context.

Parameter Type Required Description project_id string required role string optional Controls verbosity. 'worker'=sprint+tasks only, 'planner'=full context.

Example: 

get_session_brief(project_id="abc-123")

docs.title: log_task
docs.text:
Log what this session did, is doing, or failed at. Call frequently — this is the primary signal in the timeline and handoffs.

Valid statuses: pending · in_progress · done · failed · backlog · future · backburner

Parameter Type Required Description session_id string required project_id string required description string required status string optional

Example: 

log_task(session_id="session-uuid", project_id="abc-123", description="Fixed auth bug", status="done")

docs.title: get_tasks
docs.text:
Get recent tasks across all sessions.

Parameter Type Required Description project_id string required limit integer optional

Example: 

get_tasks(project_id="abc-123")

docs.title: search_tasks
docs.text:
Search tasks by keyword or natural-language query. Uses trigram similarity on Postgres, LIKE on SQLite. Returns top matches with similarity score.

Parameter Type Required Description project_id string required query string required limit integer optional

Example: 

search_tasks(project_id="abc-123", query="rate limiting bug")

docs.title: get_goal
docs.text:
Read the current goal state.

Parameter Type Required Description project_id string required

Example: 

get_goal(project_id="abc-123")

docs.title: set_goal
docs.text:
Set or update the goal state.

Parameter Type Required Description project_id string required content string required

Example: 

set_goal(project_id="abc-123", content="Build a great product")

docs.title: pin_decision
docs.text:
Record an authoritative decision that supersedes earlier statements. Pinned decisions appear in every session's context block.

Categories: STRATEGIC · COMPETITIVE · TECHNICAL · TACTICAL · BUSINESS · PRODUCT · ARCHITECTURAL

Parameter Type Required Description project_id string required title string required body string required category string optional

Example: 

pin_decision(project_id="abc-123", title="Use psycopg3", body="asyncpg has DLL issues on Windows", category="TECHNICAL")

docs.title: update_decision
docs.text:
Patch a pinned decision. Pass new_title + new_body to atomically supersede (creates a new row, marks old as superseded). Otherwise patches in place.

Parameter Type Required Description decision_id string required new_title string optional new_body string optional title string optional body string optional category string optional status string optional

docs.title: get_pinned_decisions
docs.text:
List pinned decisions (active only by default, newest first).

Parameter Type Required Description project_id string required include_superseded boolean optional

Example: 

get_pinned_decisions(project_id="abc-123")

docs.title: request_hitl
docs.text:
Surface a question to the human queue. urgency='blocking' pauses the session until answered — poll get_hitl_request to resume. normal/high land in the dashboard without blocking.

Parameter Type Required Description project_id string required question string required session_id string optional context string optional urgency string optional assigned_to string optional

Example: 

request_hitl(project_id="abc-123", question="Should we add rate limiting here?", urgency="normal")

docs.title: get_hitl_request
docs.text:
Poll a HITL request for the human's answer. Returns the row including status (pending/answered/dismissed) and answer text.

Parameter Type Required Description request_id string required

Example: 

get_hitl_request(request_id="hitl-uuid")

docs.title: generate_handoff
docs.text:
Generate a context handoff document. mode='full' writes the complete L0/L1/L2 handoff. mode='delta' returns a compact session summary with completed items, pending items, and the next /goal string.

Parameter Type Required Description project_id string required mode string optional session_id string optional Optional session id for auto-delta on repeated calls in the same session.

Example: 

generate_handoff(project_id="abc-123", mode="delta", session_id="session-uuid")

docs.title: get_context_block
docs.text:
Return a compact plain-text context block (north star, sprint, pending sprint items, recent tasks, recent decisions, active sessions). Use mode='full' to paste into a fresh Claude Code session; mode='chat' for a shorter paste into claude.ai.

Parameter Type Required Description project_id string required mode string optional

Example: 

get_context_block(project_id="abc-123", mode="chat")

docs.title: add_note
docs.text:
Add a per-project wiki note. Use for setup instructions, gotchas, environment details, how-tos.

Parameter Type Required Description project_id string required title string required body string required tags string optional

Example: 

add_note(project_id="abc-123", title="Deploy note", body="Reminder: update env vars before deploy", tags="ops,deploy")

docs.title: get_notes
docs.text:
List project notes (newest first). Filter by tag substring.

Parameter Type Required Description project_id string required tag string optional

Example: 

get_notes(project_id="abc-123")

docs.title: delete_note
docs.text:
Hard-delete a project note by id.

Parameter Type Required Description note_id string required

docs.title: create_project
docs.text:
Create a new Meridian project.

Parameter Type Required Description name string required

Example: 

create_project(name="my-app")

docs.title: register_session
docs.text:
Deprecated

Use start_session instead — it registers the session and returns goal + context in one call.

Parameter Type Required Description project_id string required session_name string required human_id string optional client string optional

Example: 

register_session(project_id="abc-123", session_name="feature-x", human_id="alice")

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: Get Meridian running and connected to Claude Code in under 10 minutes.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.11+ (check: python --version)
  • One of: pixi, pip, or Docker
  • A Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor, or Windsurf installation

docs.title: Step 1 — Install Meridian
docs.text:
pixi (recommended)pipDocker Compose

pixi is the fastest way to install Meridian with all dependencies locked.

git clone https://github.com/meridianmcp/Meridian
cd Meridian
pixi install

Start the server: 

pixi run start
# → Meridian running on http://127.0.0.1:7878


git clone https://github.com/meridianmcp/Meridian
cd Meridian
pip install -e ".[full]"
python -m meridian
# → Meridian running on http://127.0.0.1:7878

git clone https://github.com/meridianmcp/Meridian
cd Meridian
docker compose up
# → Meridian running on http://localhost:7878

Data is persisted in ./data/meridian.db via volume mount.

Confirm it's running: 

curl http://localhost:7878/health
# → {"status": "ok", "version": "1.9.0", "db": "sqlite"}

Open the dashboard: http://localhost:7878

docs.title: Step 2 — Create your first project
docs.text:
In the dashboard, click New Project and give it a name (e.g. my-project).

Or via the MCP tools (after Step 3): 

create_project(name="my-project")

docs.title: Claude Code
docs.text:
Add to .mcp.json in your project root (or ~/.claude/mcp.json globally):

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "meridian": {
      "command": "pixi",
      "args": ["run", "python", "-m", "meridian", "--mcp"],
      "cwd": "/absolute/path/to/Meridian"
    }
  }
}

Tip

Replace /absolute/path/to/Meridian with the actual path where you cloned the repo. On Windows use forward slashes: C:/Users/you/Meridian.

docs.title: Claude Desktop
docs.text:
Add to ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json (macOS) or %APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json (Windows):

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "meridian": {
      "command": "pixi",
      "args": ["run", "python", "-m", "meridian", "--mcp"],
      "cwd": "/absolute/path/to/Meridian"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Cursor
docs.text:
Add to .cursor/mcp.json in your project root:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "meridian": {
      "command": "pixi",
      "args": ["run", "python", "-m", "meridian", "--mcp"],
      "cwd": "/absolute/path/to/Meridian"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Windsurf
docs.text:
Add to ~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "meridian": {
      "command": "pixi",
      "args": ["run", "python", "-m", "meridian", "--mcp"],
      "cwd": "/absolute/path/to/Meridian"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Step 4 — Your first coordinated session
docs.text:
Start a session in Claude Code. Meridian tools will be available automatically.

Paste this into your first Claude Code message:

start_session(
  project_id="<your-project-id>",
  session_name="my-first-session"
)
get_goal(project_id="<your-project-id>")

Finding your project ID

Open the Meridian dashboard at http://localhost:7878, select your project, and copy the ID from the URL or project settings.

docs.title: Step 5 — Use Meridian in your workflow
docs.text:
Once connected, add these calls to your Claude prompts:

Start of session: 

start_session(project_id="...", session_name="feature-auth-fix")

Log progress: 

log_task(session_id="...", project_id="...",
  description="Fixed JWT refresh token bug — tokens now invalidated on rotation",
  status="done")

When context is filling up: 

generate_handoff(project_id="...")
Then start a new Claude Code session and paste the handoff file.

See what other sessions are doing: 

get_tasks(project_id="...", limit=20)

docs.title: MCP tools not showing up in Claude Code
docs.text:
1. Make sure the Meridian server is running (pixi run start)
  2. Verify the cwd path in your .mcp.json is correct and uses absolute path
  3. Restart Claude Code after editing .mcp.json
  4. Check Claude Code's MCP panel (⚙️ → MCP) for error messages

docs.title: Server won't start — port already in use
docs.text:
# Change the port
MERIDIAN_PORT=7879 pixi run start

Update your .mcp.json if you use a non-default port (MCP stdio mode doesn't use HTTP, so port only matters for the dashboard).

docs.title: Database error on startup
docs.text:
Meridian creates ./data/meridian.db automatically. If you see permission errors:

mkdir -p data && chmod 755 data
pixi run start

docs.title: Windows PATH too long error
docs.text:
This happens after many restarts on Windows. Fix:

# Run from a fresh terminal (not nested inside pixi)
python -m meridian

docs.title: Can't find project ID
docs.text:
Open http://localhost:7878, click your project, and look at the URL: http://localhost:7878/dashboard#project-<uuid> — the UUID is your project ID.

Or use the MCP tool: 

get_project_by_name(name="my-project")

docs.title: Postgres connection failing
docs.text:
Set MERIDIAN_DB_URL to your Postgres connection string:

export MERIDIAN_DB_URL="postgresql://user:pass@host/dbname"
pixi run start

Meridian auto-detects Postgres vs SQLite from the URL scheme.

docs.title: Self-Hosting
docs.text: Run Meridian on your own infrastructure. Choose the setup that fits your team.

docs.title: Option 1: Docker Compose (Recommended)
docs.text: The simplest production-ready setup. Persists data to a local volume.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: • Docker and Docker Compose v2

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/meridianmcp/Meridian
cd Meridian

Start the server: 

docker compose up -d

Verify it's running: 

curl http://localhost:7878/health
# → {"status": "ok", "version": "1.9.0", "db": "sqlite"}

The dashboard is at http://localhost:7878.

docs.title: Data persistence
docs.text:
By default, the SQLite database is stored at ./data/meridian.db via a volume mount.

# docker-compose.yml (relevant section)
volumes:
  - ./data:/app/data

docs.title: Environment variables
docs.text:
Create .env in the project root:

# Optional: use Postgres instead of SQLite
# MERIDIAN_DB_URL=postgresql://user:pass@host/dbname

# Set a secure session secret
SESSION_SECRET=your-long-random-secret-here

# Optional: password gate for preview deployments
# SITE_PASSWORD=preview-password

Restart after changing .env: 

docker compose down && docker compose up -d

docs.title: Option 2: pixi run start (Development)
docs.text:
Best for local development and testing.

git clone https://github.com/meridianmcp/Meridian
cd Meridian
pixi install
pixi run start

The server starts on http://127.0.0.1:7878.

For hot-reload during development: 

pixi run dev
# Uses uvicorn --reload, watches meridian/ for changes

docs.title: Option 3: Manual pip Install
docs.text:
For environments where pixi isn't available.

git clone https://github.com/meridianmcp/Meridian
cd Meridian
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate   # Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate
pip install -e ".[full]"
python -m meridian

docs.title: Postgres Setup with Neon (Free)
docs.text:
Neon has a generous free tier — sufficient for personal use and small teams.

  1. Create a free account at neon.tech
  2. Create a new project
  3. Copy the connection string from the Neon dashboard
  4. Set the env var:
export MERIDIAN_DB_URL="postgresql://neondb_owner:...@ep-xxx.us-east-2.aws.neon.tech/neondb?sslmode=require"
pixi run start

Meridian auto-detects Postgres from the URL prefix and uses asyncpg instead of aiosqlite.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
See the full Configuration Reference.

Key variables for self-hosting:

Variable What to set SESSION_SECRET Long random string — used to sign session cookies APP_URL Your public URL (e.g. https://meridian.yourdomain.com) MERIDIAN_DB_URL Postgres URL (optional — SQLite is fine for small teams) SITE_PASSWORD One-time password gate for private previews

docs.title: nginx
docs.text:
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name meridian.yourdomain.com;
    return 301 https://$host$request_uri;
}

server {
    listen 443 ssl http2;
    server_name meridian.yourdomain.com;

    ssl_certificate     /etc/letsencrypt/live/meridian.yourdomain.com/fullchain.pem;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/meridian.yourdomain.com/privkey.pem;

    location / {
        proxy_pass         http://127.0.0.1:7878;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header   Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header   Connection "upgrade";
        proxy_set_header   Host $host;
        proxy_set_header   X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header   X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header   X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
        proxy_read_timeout 3600s;   # needed for WebSocket connections
    }
}

WebSocket support

Meridian uses WebSockets for the live dashboard. The Upgrade and Connection headers are required — don't remove them.

docs.title: Caddy
docs.text:
meridian.yourdomain.com {
    reverse_proxy localhost:7878 {
        header_up Host {host}
        header_up X-Forwarded-For {remote}
        header_up X-Forwarded-Proto {scheme}
    }
}

Caddy handles SSL automatically via Let's Encrypt.

docs.title: Upgrading
docs.text:
cd Meridian
git pull origin main
pixi install        # or: docker compose build
pixi run start      # or: docker compose up -d

Meridian runs all database migrations automatically on startup — no manual SQL needed.

docs.title: SQLite
docs.text:
# Backup
cp data/meridian.db "data/meridian-$(date +%Y%m%d).db"

# Restore
cp data/meridian-20260101.db data/meridian.db

For automated backups: 

# Add to crontab — daily backup, keep last 7
0 2 * * * cp /path/to/Meridian/data/meridian.db \
  /backups/meridian-$(date +%Y%m%d).db && \
  find /backups -name "meridian-*.db" -mtime +7 -delete

docs.title: Postgres (Neon)
docs.text:
Neon handles backups automatically with point-in-time recovery. Manual backup:

pg_dump "$MERIDIAN_DB_URL" -Fc -f meridian-backup-$(date +%Y%m%d).dump
# Restore:
pg_restore -d "$MERIDIAN_DB_URL" meridian-backup-20260101.dump

docs.title: Running Multiple Instances
docs.text:
SQLite doesn't support multiple writers

If you need multiple Meridian instances (for load balancing or HA), use Postgres. SQLite will work with multiple readers but not concurrent writers.

docs.title: With Postgres + multiple instances
docs.text:
# docker-compose.yml with 2 instances
services:
  meridian-1:
    build: .
    ports: ["7878:7878"]
    environment:
      - MERIDIAN_DB_URL=postgresql://...
  meridian-2:
    build: .
    ports: ["7879:7878"]
    environment:
      - MERIDIAN_DB_URL=postgresql://...

Use nginx upstream to load balance between them.
